Because Of the dispute over the re-
moval of the dead tyee to the Idaho
burying grounds, 1t is likely that .
council will be called a,d the ques-
tion discussed by the older men and

The removal of Joseph to Idaho
would mean a great expense and an
outpouring OF Indians on the trails 70
Idaho such as has never been equaled
in these later years.


Owing to the condition OF the
corpse, which is merely enclosed In .
pine box. weighted with boards
and stones and covered with sand to
protect 1t from the coyotes, which
roam the hill on which the burial
ground IL located. no railroad COM
pany would accept II for shipment
For this reason II would be neces-
sary to take the chief overland on q
march something over l60 milea
Should this be done q guard of honor
consisting Of every Nez Perces brave,
kluchman and pappoose on the reser-
walton would follow the great fyee.
Bands Of Indians from all the other
tribes would also be invited to accom-
pany the procession. 1t would b an


event TO give the braves almost ur-


licensed freedom on the route unless


restrained by an escort OF troops.
WILLIAM FRANCIS GUIoN.
